text_original: When a sage rales a state the benefits of the state will be increased twice.
text_normalized: When a sage rales a state the benefits of the state will be increased twice.

text_original: When he governs the empire, those of the empire will be doubled.
text_normalized: When he governs the empire, those of the empire will be doubled.

text_original: This increase is not by appropriating land from without.
text_normalized: This increase is not by appropriating land from without.

text_original: But by cutting out the useless expenditures it is accomplished.
text_normalized: But by cutting out the useless expenditures it is accomplished.

text_original: In issuing an order, taldng up an enterprise, or employing the people and expending wealth, the sage never does an3d3hing without some useful purpose.
text_normalized: In issuing an order, taldng up an enterprise, or employing the people and expending wealth, the sage never does an3d3hing without some useful purpose.

text_original: Therefore wealth is not wasted and people’s resources are not exhausted, and many are the blessings procured.
text_normalized: Therefore wealth is not wasted and people’s resources are not exhausted, and many are the blessings procured.

text_original: In making clothing, what is the purpose ?
text_normalized: In making clothing, what is the purpose ?

text_original: It is to keep out the cold in winter and heat in summer.
text_normalized: It is to keep out the cold in winter and heat in summer.

text_original: The good of clothing is measured in terms of the amount of warmth it adds in winter and coolness in summer : what is merely decorative and does not contribute to these is to be let alone.
text_normalized: The good of clothing is measured in terms of the amount of warmth it adds in winter and coolness in summer : what is merely decorative and does not contribute to these is to be let alone.

text_original: In building palaces and houses, what is the purpose ?
text_normalized: In building palaces and houses, what is the purpose ?

text_original: It is to keep out the wind and the cold in winter and heat and rain in summer, and to fortify against thieves : what is merely decorative and contributes nothing to these should be let alone.
text_normalized: It is to keep out the wind and the cold in winter and heat and rain in summer, and to fortify against thieves : what is merely decorative and contributes nothing to these should be let alone.

text_original: In forging armour and shields and the five weapons, what is the purpose ?
text_normalized: In forging armour and shields and the five weapons, what is the purpose ?

text_original: It is to prepare against invasions and revolts, bandits and thieves.
text_normalized: It is to prepare against invasions and revolts, bandits and thieves.

text_original: When these happen, with armour, shields, and the five weapons one will succeed, without them one cannot succeed.
text_normalized: When these happen, with armour, shields, and the five weapons one will succeed, without them one cannot succeed.

text_original: Therefore the sages forged armour, shields, and the five weapons.
text_normalized: Therefore the sages forged armour, shields, and the five weapons.

text_original: And their good is measured in terms of lightness, sharpness and unbreakahility : what is merely decorative and contributes nothing to these should be let alone.
text_normalized: And their good is measured in terms of lightness, sharpness and unbreakahility : what is merely decorative and contributes nothing to these should be let alone.

text_original: In building boats and vehicles, what is the purpose ?
text_normalized: In building boats and vehicles, what is the purpose ?

text_original: A vehicle is to go on land and a boat is to sail rivers and valleys in order to transport the wealth in the four quarters.
text_normalized: A vehicle is to go on land and a boat is to sail rivers and valleys in order to transport the wealth in the four quarters.

text_original: The good of boats and vehicles is measured in terms of the amount of facility and convenience they afford: what is merely decorative and contributes nothing to these should be let alone.
text_normalized: The good of boats and vehicles is measured in terms of the amount of facility and convenience they afford: what is merely decorative and contributes nothing to these should be let alone.

text_original: Now, in making all these things none is done without its use in view.
text_normalized: Now, in making all these things none is done without its use in view.

text_original: Therefore wealth is not wasted and people’s resources are not exhausted, and many are the blessings procured.
text_normalized: Therefore wealth is not wasted and people’s resources are not exhausted, and many are the blessings procured.

text_original: Besides, (the sages) again gave up all such hobbies as collecting pearls and jade, and pet birds, and animals such as dogs and horses, in order to replenish clothing, houses, armour, shields, and the five weapons, and boats and vehicles.
text_normalized: Besides, (the sages) again gave up all such hobbies as collecting pearls and jade, and pet birds, and animals such as dogs and horses, in order to replenish clothing, houses, armour, shields, and the five weapons, and boats and vehicles.

text_original: And these were multiplied several times.
text_normalized: And these were multiplied several times.

text_original: But to increase these is not difficult.
text_normalized: But to increase these is not difficult.

text_original: What, then, is difficult to increase ?
text_normalized: What, then, is difficult to increase ?

text_original: To increase the population is difficult.
text_normalized: To increase the population is difficult.

text_original: In ancient times, the sage-kings said : '' No man of twenty should dare to be without a family ; no girl of fifteen should dare to be without a master.” Such were the laws of the sage-kings.
text_normalized: In ancient times, the sage-kings said : '' No man of twenty should dare to be without a family ; no girl of fifteen should dare to be without a master.” Such were the laws of the sage-kings.

text_original: Now that the sage-kings have passed away, the people have become loose.
text_normalized: Now that the sage-kings have passed away, the people have become loose.

text_original: Those who like to have a family early sometimes marry at twenty.
text_normalized: Those who like to have a family early sometimes marry at twenty.

text_original: Those who like ECONOMY OF EXPENDITURES (I) 119 to liave a family late sometimes marry at forty.
text_normalized: Those who like ECONOMY OF EXPENDITURES (I) 119 to liave a family late sometimes marry at forty.

text_original: Wlien tlie late marriages are made up by the early ones, (tbe average) is still later than the legal requirements of the sage-kings by ten years.
text_normalized: Wlien tlie late marriages are made up by the early ones, (tbe average) is still later than the legal requirements of the sage-kings by ten years.

text_original: Supposing births are given to children on an average of one in three years, then two or three children should have been born (by the time men now marry).
text_normalized: Supposing births are given to children on an average of one in three years, then two or three children should have been born (by the time men now marry).

text_original: This is not just to urge men to establish families early in order to increase the population.
text_normalized: This is not just to urge men to establish families early in order to increase the population.

text_original: Those who govern the empire to-day diminish the people in more ways than one : Employing the people they exhaust them, levying taxes they make them heavy.
text_normalized: Those who govern the empire to-day diminish the people in more ways than one : Employing the people they exhaust them, levying taxes they make them heavy.

text_original: People fall into poverty and innumerable persons die of hunger and cold.
text_normalized: People fall into poverty and innumerable persons die of hunger and cold.

text_original: Moreover the rulers make war and attack some neighbouring states.
text_normalized: Moreover the rulers make war and attack some neighbouring states.

text_original: It may last a whole year, or, at the shortest, several months.
text_normalized: It may last a whole year, or, at the shortest, several months.

text_original: Thus man and woman cannot see each other for a long time.
text_normalized: Thus man and woman cannot see each other for a long time.

text_original: Is not this a way to diminish the people?
text_normalized: Is not this a way to diminish the people?

text_original: Living in danger, eating and drinking irregularly many become sick and die.
text_normalized: Living in danger, eating and drinking irregularly many become sick and die.

text_original: Hiding in ambush, setting fire, besieging a city, and battling in the open fields, innumerable men die.
text_normalized: Hiding in ambush, setting fire, besieging a city, and battling in the open fields, innumerable men die.

text_original: Are not ways of diminishing the people getting numerous with the government of the rulers of to-day ?
text_normalized: Are not ways of diminishing the people getting numerous with the government of the rulers of to-day ?

text_original: They did not exist when the sage-kings administered the government.
text_normalized: They did not exist when the sage-kings administered the government.

text_original: (In the government by the sagacious), many ways of increasing the population will arise.
text_normalized: (In the government by the sagacious), many ways of increasing the population will arise.

text_original: Therefore Motse said : To cut out useless expenditures is the way of the sage-kings and a great blessing to the world.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said : To cut out useless expenditures is the way of the sage-kings and a great blessing to the world.
